author: @chriskeeble
text:
Hi Rob,

Have loved watching your content over the last couple of years, but increasingly YouTube is not anyone's friend - not viewers like me and not content publishers like you.

More and more of us are leaving YouTube behind and looking at alternative content platforms such as Rumble and X (Twitter) - i.e. those platforms which don't censor / cancel  / demonetise for political reasons.

I hope you'll consider sharing your content on one of these platforms so that those of us who are migrating away from YouTube will still get to enjoy your content.

Best wishes, and thanks for all the content.

Chris

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i have been thinking about it mate

replies.author: @willhibbardii2450
replies.text: Chris, I agree... YouTube, Google, Facebook, Twitter, America Online, Prodigy, Yahoo, Apple, Microsoft. Ect... have all become advertising giants. I choose not to support them any longer. It's defiantly past time for change. Other than commenting on a few channels I've chosen not to participate providing any content for a while now. The content that I did in the past publish I expected no return compensation for. I've watched great content providers laboriously spend time developing their audiences have their content stripped and channels canceled. Robert, your content is priceless! Your labors are priceless! The community that you have developed are priceless!  Rumble & X are the alternatives however I believe that I'm going back to the basics providing my own opensource server. I don't need the cloud, AI and unsolicited advertisement or who to watch suggestions. I'm seriously considering for 2024 deleting my accounts and getting rid of the clutter and getting back to basics. The communication linking is being abused by CORPORATIONS and I'm moved to pull my plug away from it and them. The value of independent public broadcasting is needed without commercial spots.

author: @secretrat
text:
You are right that there is no free lunch. At the same time as Youtube keeps changing it's payment algorithm, I am beginning to feel assaulted from all sides. The advertising is intrusive and often poorly timed but it is what it is. All content creators from Hollywood studios to Fred the rag collector are feeling forced to divide their content into too many streams. For example at one time, Blockbuster could supply all movie content I wanted. Then there was Netflix. Then Prime. Now Hulu, Peacock, Disney Paramouint, HBO etc, all of whom want their piece of my wallet, and worse, the need to deal with 9 different platforms.

I guess I am becoming rebellious at this trend, so while I very much like your videos, I won't be adding one more leak from my wallet. I appreciate that you will keep providing advertising supported content, and if I need to wait two weeks, that's OK. Thank you for seeing this side of the coin.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i understand mate

author: @voltrevolt8731
text: For sure the ads seem to be turning YouTube into the equivalent of a radio station in the US -- five minutes of music per hour and 55 minutes of ads -- which is why I just paid for a YouTube subscription. But regarding your audience, you've spent YEARS building your channel and growing your audience and community, and you've inspired a lot of people to get involved! You truly deserve the success you're having, thanks for all your dedication and productive work.

author: @Auryn.
text:
Not exactly sure your reasoning for making several Youtube channels makes sense at all. You want to make it easier for viewers to find videos, But you have seperated and in some case duplicated them accross 3 different channels. So instead of just searching 1 channel we now have to search 3 (and if we use youtube search we end up with other channels videos too). Totally your choice but this also means your managing 3 channels instead of 1 which takes away from each imo.

The members only videos is a great idea tho, but just make sure your cohesive in releases across channels and videos and playlists to not confuse those who dont see them for 2 weeks! Also expect 2 waves of people taking about the same video 2 weeks apart which could be very funny/annoying/strange haha.

replies.text: cheers mate

author: @colrodrick8784
text:
Appreciate the explanation Rob.  I hadn’t realised that there quite so many subscribers. I usually watch your videos as I wake up before getting out of bed, on the small screen of my phone.  That is also before my eyes focus too well.

Congrats.  And it all makes perfect sense.  I understand the business side as a small business person since the early 90s, before I left my real job running IT projects for Defence here, and the small business became my dominant occupation.  I also appreciate the degree of effort in setting up the different channels to group projects together, especially.

All your videos are excellent.  Honestly though, I don’t know how you find the time.

text: I fully support Rob doing what he can to increase channel membership and make a living.  My 2¢ is that this change to the channel doesn't seem to add much value to existing members. I've noticed I stop getting notifications for comments older than 10 days, so it gives everyone a chance to participate.

The only thing that changes for existing members is we get to watch the daily video without ads (which is nice, but not a big deal to me personally). And non members have a 2 week time lag before daily videos with ads resume.  I'm still a $5 a month member... And nothing is changing about that. Have I overlooked anything?
